text.title: Audience OKR Template (Indie) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Audience OKR Template (Indie). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each segment gets exactly ONE quarterly Objective. ≥2 Objectives per segment = priorities split.
rule.rationale: Indie capacity is the constraint; forcing one Objective forces actual choice.

rule.statement: Exactly 3 Key Results per Objective: 1 growth, 1 engagement, 1 retention. KRs are numeric, dated, observable.
rule.rationale: <3 = too narrow to triangulate; >3 = nothing is a priority. Mix prevents tunnel-vision.

rule.statement: Weekly OKR check-in (≤15 min): 1-row update per KR — current value, delta, on-track/at-risk/behind.
rule.rationale: Quarterly OKRs without weekly check-in drift; check-in catches drift in week 3, not week 13.

rule.statement: KRs must be outcome metrics, not feature ship counts ('launched X', 'shipped Y'). Feature ship is not the goal.
rule.rationale: Output-shaped KRs replicate roadmap; KRs exist to measure audience-side impact.

rule.statement: End of quarter: grade each KR 0.0-1.0; OKR retro names ≥1 promote/demote/retire decision.
rule.rationale: Without grading + retro, OKRs are theatre; grading creates the learning loop.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-audience-okr-template-indie.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
